title: Introduction & ADCS Fundamentals
html:
Active Directory Certificate Services (AD CS) is Microsoft's on-premises PKI solution. Certificates can be used as password-equivalent credentials, enabling authentication as any user — including Domain Admins. This reference covers all known ADCS ESC (Escalation) attack techniques, ESC1 through ESC16, demonstrated against the GOAD lab (essos.local domain, CA: ESSOS-CA on braavos.essos.local).

Quick Enumeration

Tip

Run this first to discover ADCS servers and the CA name.

# Discover ADCS servers
nxc ldap <dc-ip> -u '<username>' -p '<password>' -M adcs
# Discover ADCS servers in the environment
nxc ldap 192.168.56.10-23 -u '' -p '' -M adcs

SMB         192.168.56.12   445    MEEREEN          [*] Windows 10.0 Build 17763 x64 (name:MEEREEN) (domain:essos.local) (signing:True) (SMBv1:False)
LDAPS       192.168.56.12   636    MEEREEN          [+] essos.local\guest:
ADCS        192.168.56.12   -      MEEREEN          Found PKI Enrollment Server: meereen.essos.local
ADCS        192.168.56.12   -      MEEREEN          Found CN=ESSOS-CA,CN=Enrollment Services,...

SMB         192.168.56.23   445    BRAAVOS          [*] Windows 10.0 Build 17763 x64 (name:BRAAVOS) (domain:essos.local)
LDAPS       192.168.56.23   636    BRAAVOS          [+] essos.local\guest:
ADCS        192.168.56.23   -      BRAAVOS          Found PKI Enrollment Server: braavos.essos.local
ADCS        192.168.56.23   -      BRAAVOS          Found CN=ESSOS-CA,CN=Enrollment Services,...

Full Vulnerable Template Enumeration

# Certify — find all vulnerable templates
Certify.exe find /vulnerable /enabled

# Certipy — comprehensive scan
certipy find -u <username>@<domain> -p <password> -dc-ip <dc-ip> -target <ca-host> -stdout

ESC Attack Summary

  • ESC1 — Misconfigured Certificate Templates — enrollee supplies SAN
  • ESC2 — Any Purpose EKU template — weaker than ESC1 alone
  • ESC3 — Enrollment Agent Templates — request on behalf of any user
  • ESC4 — Vulnerable Certificate Template ACL — WriteProperty on template
  • ESC5 — Vulnerable PKI Object ACL — WriteProperty on CA object
  • ESC6 — EDITF_ATTRIBUTESUBJECTALTNAME2 — CA-wide SAN flag
  • ESC7 — Vulnerable CA ACL — ManageCA or ManageCertificates
  • ESC8 — NTLM Relay to AD CS HTTP Enrollment endpoint
  • ESC9 — NO_SECURITY_EXTENSION — certificates survive password changes
  • ESC10 — Weak Certificate Mappings — altSecurityIdentities abuse
  • ESC11 — IF_ENFORCEENCRYPTICERTREQUEST cleared — DCOM/RPC relay
  • ESC12 — Shell access to CA server — Golden Certificate
  • ESC13 — Issuance Policy OID Group Links — group membership in TGT
  • ESC14 — Shadow Credentials + altSecurityIdentities mapping
  • ESC15 — Version 1 Template Application Policies (CVE-2024-49019)
  • ESC16 — CA-Wide Security Extension Removal — omits szOID_NTDS_CA_SECURITY_EXT

Lab Credentials (GOAD)

  • missandei:fr3edom — Domain User (essos.local)
  • khal.drogo:horse — Domain User with ManageCA/WriteProperty
  • viserys.targaryen — ManageCA + ManageCertificates on ESSOS-CA
  • DC: 192.168.56.12 (meereen.essos.local)
  • CA: 192.168.56.23 (braavos.essos.local)
excerpt: Active Directory Certificate Services (AD CS) is Microsoft's on-premises PKI solution. Certificates can be used as password-equivalent credentials, enabling authentication as any user — including Doma
